cases.context: A SaaS startup grew from 8 to 25 engineers in 6 months after a Series A.
cases.previous_state: Maintained a single flat engineering team. Deployment frequency plummeted due to merge conflicts and lack of clear ownership over distinct product areas.
cases.applied_solution: Transitioned to `Stream-Aligned + Early Platform`. Divided into 3 stream-aligned product squads and assigned 2 senior engineers to standardize the terraform modules and CI pipelines (Early Platform).
cases.results: Deployment frequency recovered to 3x/day. Cognitive load on individual engineers dropped as they no longer manually maintained infrastructure.

cases.context: A neobank with 150 engineers running the 'Spotify Model'.
cases.previous_state: High autonomy led to 5 different programming languages and 12 different ways of deploying to AWS. Security audits were failing. Cross-team collaboration was blocked by architectural mismatch.
cases.applied_solution: Implemented `Compliance-Enforced Platform`. Stripped infrastructural autonomy from squads. Mandated consumption of a centralized Internal Developer Platform (IDP) that automatically handled SOC2 logging and mTLS.
cases.results: Architectural drift reduced to zero on new services. Security audits passed automatically due to Compliance-as-Code constraints built into the platform.
